title: New Deadline Looms for U.S. and Iran as Truce Wavers
summary: Fractures were already emerging in the limited cease-fire. Vice President JD Vance will lead a U.S. delegation in talks this weekend.

title: Is the Strait of Hormuz Reopening? What to Know Under Iran-Cease Fire.
summary: Vessels are wary of passing the coast of Iran in the strait, given the fragile agreement, and the number of ships traveling through it has even dropped.

title: Markets Have Faced a Year of Chaos and Still Done Awfully Well
summary: Most stock investors have lost money during the Iran war, but returns have been splendid for the year since the “Liberation Day” tariff announcement.

title: A ‘Messy’ Cease-Fire, and a 15-Year Sentence for the ‘Ketamine Queen’
summary: Plus, the latest country planning to ban social media for teens.

title: How Trump Purged Immigration Judges to Speed Up Deportations
summary: Judges are ordering an unprecedented number of people deported after coming under significant pressure from the administration to do so or risk losing their jobs.

title: In a Deep Red Town In Pennsylvania, Locals Vent Over a Planned ICE Detention Center
summary: The Tremont, Pa., area has roughly 2,000 residents and limited resources. The Trump administration plans to convert a warehouse there to hold nearly four times as many people.

title: On Truth Social, Trump Supporters Fume About Iran War
summary: A growing chorus of disaffected Trump supporters is sounding off in the replies to his posts on the social media platform he founded.

title: On Social Media, Democratic Politicians Are Letting the F-Word Fly
summary: In person, on social media and in campaign ads, Democratic politicians are swearing with glee. It is usually aimed at President Trump.

title: Clean Energy Team Wins Salt River Project Election in Arizona
summary: Proponents of renewable power will control the Phoenix area utility’s policymaking for the first time after they won an unusually contentious race that drew attention from national groups.

title: No Charges for Wisconsin Mayor Who Removed Ballot Drop Box in 2024
summary: A special prosecutor said a review of the incident found there was not sufficient evidence to charge Doug Diny, the mayor of Wausau.

title: U.S. Fertility Rates Drop to Another Record Low
summary: The fertility rate has been falling since 2007, in large part because of a plunge among teenagers.

title: A Must for the Next Food Craze? Be ‘Social Media Gorgeous.’
summary: The ascent of ube has little to do with the purple yam’s taste or Filipino origins. It’s the color, flavor experts say.

title: White House Secures Foreign Steel for Trump’s Ballroom Project
summary: ArcelorMittal, a European steel maker, is donating tens of millions of dollars of foreign steel for President Trump’s new ballroom.

title: Climate Change Denial Sees a Resurgence in Trump’s Washington
summary: A conference near the White House drew dozens of people who reject the scientific consensus on climate change. The mood was triumphant.

title: Mamdani Asserts Control Over N.Y.P.D., Saying He Would Overrule Tisch
summary: Although Mayor Zohran Mamdani often seems to defer to his police commissioner, Jessica Tisch, he insisted that he had the final say in policing matters.

title: Mamdani Acknowledges ‘Troubling’ Job Losses in 100 Days’ Interview
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is focused on economic justice. Some budget watchdogs and business leaders want him to concentrate on economic development.

title: Gilgo Beach Killer’s ‘Unusual’ Deal Could Help Solve Other Murders
summary: Rex Heuermann agreed to talk to behavioral analysts at the F.B.I. when he pleaded guilty. The unit has interviewed Ted Bundy, John Wayne Gacy and Richard Speck.

title: In 20 Minutes, Gilgo Beach Killer Admits to Murdering 8 Women
summary: As he pleaded guilty on Wednesday, Rex Heuermann gave the same one-word answer each time he was asked how he killed one of his victims.

title: How a New York Times Reporter Covers the Illegal Drug Trade
summary: A new series on dangerous synthetic drugs called for sources on the other side of the law.

title: Fed’s Inflation Woes Preceded the War With Iran
summary: An energy shock stemming from the fighting has added a layer of complexity to the Federal Reserve’s decision-making around interest rates.

title: Blue Collar Work Has Plateaued, Narrowing Options for Young Workers
summary: Skilled electricians, plumbers and factory workers are in demand, but job openings have dropped.

title: How War in the Middle East Paralyzed an Asian Food Giant
summary: Vietnam, the world’s No. 2 rice exporter, cut production as power prices surged. Even with a temporary cease-fire in Iran, worries linger over the world’s food supply.

title: The First Race of the L.A. Olympics: Buying Tickets
summary: The winners of a lottery for a presale prioritizing locals often came away with sticker shock. Still, organizers said early sales had “significantly exceeded” those of other Games.

title: These Homesteaders Prep for the End of the World at Oklahoma Expo
summary: Homesteading, for all its bucolic imagery, taps into the desire to escape from the disquiet of modern America, where anything can happen.

title: NASA Prepares for Artemis II Splashdown After Historic Moon Flyby
summary: The mission is seen as a key step toward resuming crewed lunar landings. Artemis II’s four astronauts are scheduled to land in the waters off Southern California on Friday.

title: Hillary Clinton: How to Fix Affordability
summary: Our children will pay the price for the president’s indifference.

title: How Ben Sasse Is Living Now That He Is Dying
summary: The former senator wants to heal the America he’s leaving behind.

title: Stephen Colbert Skewers Trump’s ‘Double-Sided’ Cease-Fire
summary: “The Late Show” host asked if being double-sided wasn’t a prerequisite: “I believe there’s a word for a single-sided cease-fire and it’s ‘murder.’”

title: 3 Russian Submarines Detected Near Britain Were Spying, U.K. Says
summary: John Healey, the defense secretary, said the submarines were gathering information about crucial undersea pipelines and cables.

title: In Germany’s East, the Far Right Could Soon Take Power. This Is Its Plan.
summary: In the eastern state of Saxony-Anhalt, the Alternative for Germany could win control of the government this fall. Once in power, it has a plan to overhaul German society.

title: I.U.C.N. Red List Moves Emperor Penguins to “Endangered”
summary: Populations are declining as climate change causes the sea ice the birds need for survival to retreat, according to researchers.

title: No, Britain Is Not Having a Christian Revival
summary: A study said church attendance had soared among British young people, a trend reversal that excited religious conservatives around the world. Turns out it wasn’t true.
